questions.q: Under which Article of the Constitution is the Finance Commission constituted?
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission is constituted under Article 280 of the Constitution of India.

questions.q: How often is the Finance Commission normally constituted?
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission is normally constituted every five years, or earlier if necessary.

questions.q: What is the main task of the Finance Commission?
options: Sharing tax revenues between the Union and states
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission recommends how tax revenues are to be distributed between the Union and the states.

questions.q: In which year was the first Finance Commission constituted?
questions.explanation: The first Finance Commission was constituted in 1951.

questions.q: Who appoints the Finance Commission?
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission is constituted by the President of India under Article 280.

questions.q: The Finance Commission is best described as which type of body?
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission is a constitutional body established under Article 280 of the Constitution.

questions.q: How many members, including the Chairman, does the Finance Commission consist of?
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission consists of a Chairman and four other members appointed by the President.

questions.q: The division of tax proceeds between the Union and the states is broadly referred to as:
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission recommends the devolution of central taxes to the states.

questions.q: The Finance Commission's recommendations are addressed primarily to which two levels of government?
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission's core mandate is the financial relationship between the Centre and the states.

questions.q: Grants given by the Centre to states based on Finance Commission recommendations under Article 275 are called:
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission recommends grants-in-aid to states under Article 275 of the Constitution.

questions.q: Who served as the Chairman of the first Finance Commission?
questions.explanation: K.C. Neogy was the Chairman of the first Finance Commission constituted in 1951.

questions.q: Article 280 specifies that the Finance Commission shall recommend the distribution of the net proceeds of which taxes?
options: Taxes to be divided between the Union and the states
questions.explanation: Article 280 mandates the Commission to recommend the distribution of divisible taxes between the Union and the states.

questions.q: Are the recommendations of the Finance Commission binding on the government?
questions.explanation: The recommendations of the Finance Commission are advisory and not binding on the government.

questions.q: The qualifications of the members of the Finance Commission are determined by:
questions.explanation: Article 280 empowers Parliament to determine by law the qualifications and manner of selection of Commission members.

questions.q: Which Finance Commission's recommendations covered the period beginning 2021-22?
questions.explanation: The Fifteenth Finance Commission's recommendations applied to the period from 2021-22.

questions.q: Apart from the Chairman, members of the Finance Commission are generally chosen for their expertise in which field?
questions.explanation: Members are typically experts in finance, economics, public administration and related fields.

questions.q: The Finance Commission also recommends measures to augment the resources of which local-level bodies?
questions.explanation: Since the 73rd and 74th Amendments, the Commission recommends measures to augment the consolidated funds of states to support panchayats and municipalities.

questions.q: The proportion of central taxes devolved to states recommended by the Fourteenth Finance Commission was increased to:
questions.explanation: The Fourteenth Finance Commission raised the states' share of the divisible pool of central taxes to 42 percent.

questions.q: Who chaired the Fifteenth Finance Commission?
questions.explanation: N.K. Singh chaired the Fifteenth Finance Commission.

questions.q: The Finance Commission submits its report to whom?
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission submits its report to the President, who lays it before Parliament.

questions.q: The body of finances dealt with by the Finance Commission falls under which broad aspect of governance?
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission is central to India's fiscal federalism, balancing Centre-state financial relations.

questions.q: The report of the Finance Commission, when laid before Parliament, is accompanied by an explanatory memorandum on the:
questions.explanation: The report is laid before Parliament along with an explanatory memorandum on the action taken on its recommendations.

questions.q: Who chaired the Fourteenth Finance Commission?
questions.explanation: Former RBI Governor Y.V. Reddy chaired the Fourteenth Finance Commission.

questions.q: The vertical devolution recommended by a Finance Commission refers to the sharing of taxes between:
options: The Union and the states collectively
questions.explanation: Vertical devolution refers to the division of the divisible pool between the Union and the states as a whole.

questions.q: The horizontal devolution formula used by the Finance Commission distributes funds:
questions.explanation: Horizontal devolution refers to the distribution of the states' share among the individual states using a formula.

questions.q: Which Finance Commission was given the additional task of considering the impact of the Goods and Services Tax on the finances of governments?
questions.explanation: The Fifteenth Finance Commission examined the implications of GST, which had been introduced in 2017.

questions.q: Which census year's population data was the Fifteenth Finance Commission directed to use as the primary basis in its formula?
questions.explanation: The Fifteenth Finance Commission was directed to use the 2011 census population data in its formula.

questions.q: The Sixteenth Finance Commission, constituted in late 2023, was chaired by which economist?
questions.explanation: Arvind Panagariya was appointed Chairman of the Sixteenth Finance Commission, constituted in 2023.

questions.q: Before the introduction of GST, a major divisible tax shared on the recommendation of early Finance Commissions was the:
questions.explanation: Income tax and union excise duties were among the principal divisible taxes handled by the early Finance Commissions.

questions.q: The Eightieth Amendment to the Constitution gave effect to recommendations of which Finance Commission regarding an 'alternative scheme' of devolution?
questions.explanation: The 80th Amendment of 2000 implemented the alternative scheme of devolution recommended by the Tenth Finance Commission.
